cards.back: Agents that perceive their environment and act accordingly to achieve a goal or set of goals
The ability to make decisions based on rational criteria, such as maximizing utility or minimizing cost
The environment of an agent can be internal (e.g., beliefs, desires) or external (e.g., physical world, other agents)
Agents can be structured as simple reactive systems or complex planning and decision-making systems
Agents can be classified as simple reflex agents, model-based reflex agents, utility-based agents, or goal-based agents
